title: alchemilla arvensis subsp. floribunda
intro: Alchemilla arvensis subsp. floribunda, also known as lady's mantle, is a perennial plant species in the family Rosaceae, characterized by its delicate, rounded leaves and clusters of small, yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which make it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The medicinal properties of lady's mantle are said to benefit women's health, providing relief from menstrual cramps, heavy bleeding, and other symptoms associated with PMS, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Alchemilla arvensis subsp. floribunda can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
